## Structure

Pure 2-byte constant payload. All 80 retail observations across 17/17
captures are byte-identical:

```
[0]  0xff   CONSTANT — channel-list bitmap or "all channels" sentinel
[1]  0x00   CONSTANT — terminator / count = 0
```

Wire frame (post {@code [0x03][seq LE2]} wrapper): `33 ff 00` = 3 bytes
(1B sub-op + 2B body).

Total 0x13-wrapped datagram size: 13 bytes
(7B 0x13 wrapper + 1B 0x03 + 2B seq + 1B 0x33 + 2B body).

## Variants

No variants observed. min/avg/max all = 2 bytes payload across 80
samples in 17/17 captures.

## Observed contexts

Top markers within ±2s of emission: `AFTER_ENTER_SEWER`, `IN_WORLD`.
Fires once during the world-entry burst (per-zone) — likely "your
chat-channel subscription has been initialized to defaults". Higher
counts in long-session captures (`RETAIL_VEHICLE_DRONE` × 28,
`RETAIL_LONG_PARTY_A` × 7) suggest re-emission on zone transitions.

## Open questions

- Why 0xff specifically? Channel bitmap (bit per channel) or magic
  "default subscription set" tag? Catalog has no sample with any
  other value, so untestable from current corpus.
- Does the client require this exact payload, or is any 2B body
  acceptable? Empirically 0xff/0x00 works; alternatives untested.

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.server_udp.ChatList` — emits the verified
2-byte constant body. Pinned by
`src/test/java/server/gameserver/packets/server_udp/ChatListByteIdentityTest.java`
(3 tests). Wired in `WorldEntryEvent` and `ReadyForWorldState`
sequences.
